Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=demethylluteothin + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = luteothin + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:59064, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:142840, ChEBI:CHEBI:142870; EC=2.1.1.353; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:16292785}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:59065; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:16292785};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Methyltransferase involved in the biosynthesis of the antibiotic aureothin, a nitroaryl polyketide metabolite with antifungal, cytotoxic and insecticidal activities (PubMed:16292785). Catalyzes the methylation of demethylluteothin to luteothin (also called deoxyaureothin) (PubMed:16292785). Is specific for its gamma-pyrone substrate, and does not act on the alpha-pyrone isomer (PubMed:16292785). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:16292785}.
